title: Working Parents: The 12-Minute Evening Ritual Kids Remember Most
excerpt: A short daily reconnection habit that strengthens attachment even on packed workdays.
coverAlt: Parent and child sharing quiet evening time

content.text: Protect Connection Even on Busy Days
When workdays are packed, evenings can shrink into logistics only. A short reconnection ritual can still create deep emotional safety for children.

content.caption: Short, consistent rituals build strong attachment.

content.text: Children remember repeated presence more than occasional grand gestures. Twelve focused minutes can reset connection after a long day.
Use child-led play, reflective talk, and quiet closeness in a fixed sequence.

items: Repeat for 2 weeks before evaluating impact.

content.text: Connection is a practice, not an event. Consistent small rituals are what children carry for life.
